risks: Scientific translation risk: any observed effects in preclinical models may not translate to meaningful, durable benefits in humans.
Target/biomarker risk: uncertainty in selecting validated aging biology targets and clinically actionable biomarkers/endpoints can slow development and increase failure probability.
Safety/tolerability risk: interventions that modulate fundamental aging pathways can have off-target or long-term safety liabilities that may only emerge in extended studies.
Regulatory risk: longevity-focused programs can face unclear regulatory paths if pursuing broad aging indications rather than disease-specific indications.
Financing risk: early-stage longevity biotech programs often require significant capital for long-duration studies and may face funding cyclicality.
sources: source needed (company website, pipeline disclosures, clinical trial registry entries, press releases, SEC filings, PitchBook)
